articles.title: MP High Court Judge Cycles To Court Following PM Modi's Appeal To Cut Down Fuel Consumption
articles.summary: Justice Dwarka Dhish Bansal of the Madhya Pradesh High Court was recently seen cycling, following Prime Minister Narendra Modi's call for fuel conservation amid the rising global energy crisis linked to ongoing tensions between the US and Iran. An Instagram reel posted by Free Press Journal captured the judge riding a bicycle and sharing his thoughts on the initiative. Justice Bansal said that his public cycling is aimed at raising awareness about using cycles for short commutes of 1-2 kms whene

articles.title: Supreme Court Weekly Digest April 8 - 15, 2026
articles.summary: Administrative Discretion – Judicial Review – Held that ordinarily, the Court will not exercise the power of statutory authorities or substitute its own decision for that of the authority conferred with discretionary powers - noted that since the Governor had no occasion to examine the request or exercise discretion under the Rules, the High Court's issuance of a writ of mandamus was unwarranted. [Relied on Union of India Vs. S.B.Vohra and Ors. (2004 INSC 5); Paras 10-16] State of Uttarakhand v.

articles.title: Sabarimala Reference : Live Updates From Supreme Court 9-Judge Bench [Day 14]
articles.summary: Today is the 14th day of arguments before the 9-judge bench of the Supreme Court in the Sabarimala reference. articles.summary: The article examines the role of the Governor in appointing a Chief Minister when a single party has an absolute majority in the Assembly, citing the Sarkaria Commission Report (1983). It highlights the constitutional and procedural rules governing such situations, including the requirement for a floor test. The discussion is relevant to India's governance framework and may influence future political appointments and legislative procedures.
articles.raw_summary: As per the Sarkaria Commission Report (1983), which discusses the role of the governor, if a single party has an absolute majority in the Assembly, the leader of that party should automatically be asked to become the Chief Minister.

articles.title: Why Madras HC ordered granting full maternity leave to judicial employee for third pregnancy
articles.summary: The Madras High Court ruled that a judicial employee should receive full maternity leave for her third pregnancy, criticizing a 12-week limit as discriminatory. The court stated that suffering during pregnancy is the same regardless of the number of pregnancies and that a welfare state cannot deny equal benefits. The ruling highlights the need for equitable maternity leave policies in government employment.

The bench ruled that sharing a poetic recitation without intent to incite does not constitute promoting enmity or public mischief. The decision underscores protections for free speech in India.
articles.raw_summary: ‘Sharing a poetic recitation without any additional commentary or intent to incite, cannot be construed as promoting enmity or public mischief,’ said the bench.

articles.title: Global green shipping deal still on course, say advocates
articles.summary: The International Maritime Organization (IMO) is progressing toward a Net-Zero Framework for global shipping, despite challenges in recent talks. Advocates say the deal remains on course, but the framework is not yet finalized. The IMO, a UN agency, is working to reduce greenhouse gas emissions from ships, which could impact India's maritime trade and environmental policies.

articles.title: What the Strait of Hormuz fuel crisis means for air travel
articles.summary: A fuel crisis in the Strait of Hormuz, a key oil shipping route, threatens to disrupt jet fuel supplies and cause major flight cancellations this summer. The crisis is linked to regional tensions involving Iran. Travelers may face disruptions, and questions arise about whether travel insurance covers such cancellations. The situation could impact global fuel prices and air travel, with potential ripple effects on India's energy and aviation sectors.

articles.title: IT rules amendments may create obligations outside law: Nasscom
articles.summary: Industry body Nasscom and digital rights groups have raised concerns about draft amendments to India's IT rules, arguing they could create new obligations outside the existing IT Act framework. The amendments, if implemented, might allow advisories to become binding without parliamentary oversight, potentially leading to over-censorship. The rules' extension to ordinary users and AI-generated content has also sparked technical and legal questions. The groups are calling for revisions to ensure t
articles.raw_summary: Industry body Nasscom and digital rights groups argue that draft IT second amendment rules shouldn't create new obligations outside the IT Act's framework. Concerns are raised that advisories could become binding, bypassing parliamentary review and leading to over-censorship. The rules' extension to ordinary users and AI-generated content also sparks significant technical and legal questions.

The case involved Vedanta challenging Adani’s acquisition of Jaypee’s assets under the Insolvency and Bankruptcy Code (IBC). The tribunal emphasized that commercial wisdom, not judicial review, should guide such decisions, setting a precede
articles.raw_summary: NCLAT upholds NCLT decision, says no material irregularity in insolvency resolution process, creditors under ‘no obligation’ to approve plan with highest financial offer.

This initiative reflects NTPC's strategic shift toward nuclear energy as part of India's broader energy policy and infrastructure goals.

articles.title: Freebie burden may strain state finances, push borrowing higher: Economists
articles.summary: Economists warn that rising welfare spending by Indian states, including subsidies and freebies, could strain state finances and force cuts to capital expenditure. M R Madhavan, Co-founder of PRS Legislative Research, highlights that a large portion of state revenues is already committed to salaries, pensions, interest payments, and subsidies, leaving little room for discretionary spending. He notes this could ultimately push states to borrow more.
articles.raw_summary: M R Madhavan, Co-founder & Pres, PRS Legislative Research, and who has previously worked as an economist with I-Sec, ICICI and Bank of America, said states may ultimately be forced to squeeze capital expenditure if welfare spending rises while revenues disappoint. He noted that a large portion of state revenues is already committed towards salaries, pensions, interest payments and subsidies, leaving limited room for discretionary spending.

articles.title: India Cannot Sell Its Cotton to the World Until It Admits Why the World Stopped Buying
articles.summary: India's cotton sector has a productivity problem, a quality problem and a market-credibility problem. What the mission does not address may be more revealing than what it does.

articles.summary: Etihad Airways flight EY343 from Chennai to Abu Dhabi returned to the gate before departure due to a technical issue, not a fire as some reports suggested. Passenger safety remains the top priority.

articles.title: India’s retail inflation jumps to over one-year high at 3.48 per cent in April
articles.summary: India's retail inflation climbed to a 13-month high of 3.48% in April, primarily fueled by soaring food prices. Consumer Food Price Index accelerated to 4.20%, with rural inflation outpacing urban rates. While silver and gold jewellery saw significant price hikes, tomatoes surged, but potatoes and onions experienced deflation. This marks the second consecutive monthly rise, nearing the RBI's target.
articles.raw_summary: India's retail inflation climbed to a 13-month high of 3.48% in April, primarily fueled by soaring food prices. While silver and gold jewellery saw significant price hikes, tomatoes surged, but potatoes and onions experienced deflation. This marks the second consecutive monthly rise, nearing the RBI's target.

articles.title: Losing Rs 1,000 crore a day: Petrol minister Puri highlights mounting losses for OMCs as fuel prices remain unchanged for 4 years
articles.summary: Indian consumers are shielded from soaring global crude prices, but state fuel retailers are facing immense financial strain. Companies are losing approximately Rs 1,000 crore daily, with cumulative under-recoveries reaching nearly Rs 1.98 lakh crore. This price freeze, while ensuring supply, puts significant pressure on their balance sheets and future operations.

articles.title: Rupee hits record low of 95.55 as fraying US-Iran ceasefire keeps oil prices on the boil
articles.summary: The rupee has reached its lowest point ever against the US dollar. This decline is driven by investor concerns over escalating conflict in the Middle East. The situation has led to a surge in oil prices. This development poses economic challenges for India, which imports most of its energy needs. The rupee's new low reflects these growing worries.

articles.title: Consumer appliance manufacturer Mekr Technologies raises Rs 67 crore from Avaana Capital, Titan
articles.summary: Mekr Technologies, a consumer appliance startup, has secured Rs 67 crore in funding. This capital will boost manufacturing capacity and expand exports of Indian-made home and kitchen appliances. The company aims to reduce import dependency by indigenizing product manufacturing. Mekr partners with major brands, handling design, engineering, and assembly. This investment supports R D, supplier localization, and automation for global markets.

articles.title: Will petrol and diesel become costlier? Here's what experts are saying
articles.summary: Prime Minister Narendra Modi’s remarks on reducing fuel consumption have triggered debate over possible petrol and diesel price hikes in India as global crude oil risks rise due to the West Asia conflict. Its larvae can strip entire trees bare in weeks, posing a threat to the region's farm-based economy. The situation may require government intervention to mitigate agricultural and environmental impacts.
articles.raw_summary: The larvae of the Phalera cf. bucephala, commonly called the buff-tip moth, are voracious feeders, capable of stripping an entire tree bare in a few weeks

articles.title: Nine-year-old swept across LoC returns home, revives calls for humanitarian corridor
articles.summary: A nine-year-old child was swept across the Line of Control (LoC) and has returned home. The incident has revived demands for establishing a humanitarian corridor along the LoC to facilitate safe movement and address such cases. The child's return highlights concerns about border security and the need for policy measures to protect vulnerable individuals, particularly children, in conflict zones.

articles.title: The elusive choice in absence of collaterals
articles.summary: The article highlights that many individuals in India without collateral options like mutual funds or used cars rely solely on personal loans, which remain an elusive choice. It underscores the financial challenges faced by borrowers who lack assets to secure loans, potentially pointing to gaps in financial inclusion policies. The piece is based on observations from The Hindu Economy.
articles.raw_summary: Today, loans against mutual funds, used cars etc., are also options as collateral-based loans. But for many without collaterals, personal loans remain the only elusive choice

articles.title: OpenAI rolls out ‘Trusted Contact’ feature in ChatGPT to flag self-harm risks
articles.summary: OpenAI has introduced a new 'Trusted Contact' feature in ChatGPT that alerts designated contacts via email or SMS if the AI detects potential self-harm risks in conversations. Users can add trusted individuals to their account, who will be notified if ChatGPT flags concerning content. The feature aims to enhance user safety by involving trusted parties in monitoring high-risk interactions.
articles.raw_summary: The 'Trusted Contact' safety feature notifies the contact by email, SMS, etc., outlining their role when added by a user so that ChatGPT can detect self-harm conversations and alert them. This initiative, supported by the SEHAT mission, offers a sustainable, low-cost approach to improving public health through everyday food.

articles.title: Welfare spending surge pushes subsidy outgo to 91% of target
articles.summary: The Indian government's welfare spending has surged, with food subsidies reaching ₹1.92 trillion (85% of the revised target) and urea subsidies exceeding the annual target at ₹1.28 trillion (101% of revised estimates). Urea subsidy outgo is also higher than the previous year's ₹1.16 trillion. This reflects increased expenditure on social welfare programs, particularly food and agricultural subsidies, which may prompt policy reviews or adjustments.
articles.raw_summary: Food subsidy, the largest component, stood at ₹1.92 trillion, or 85% of the revised estimate of ₹2.28 trillion. However, the urea subsidy has already breached its annual target, reaching ₹1.28 trillion, or 101% of the revised estimates, and higher than the ₹1.16 trillion recorded a year ago.

articles.title: Moody's slashes 2026 India growth forecast to 6%
articles.summary: Moody’s Ratings has cut India’s GDP growth forecast for 2026 by 0.8 percentage points to 6 per cent, citing weak private consumption, slower capital formation and industrial activity, along with higher energy costs. It also lowered the 2027 growth outlook to 6 per cent from earlier estimates, pointing to lingering global and domestic pressures.

articles.title: China’s factory inflation surges to a near 4-year high; domestic demand remains tepid
articles.summary: China's factory prices rose by 2.8% in April, the fastest increase since July 2022, ending a period of deflation. The surge is linked to disruptions in energy supplies due to the Iran war, but weak domestic demand and a sluggish labor market are limiting firms' ability to raise prices. The situation highlights broader economic challenges in China that could affect global trade and energy markets.

articles.title: Bihar: Over 150 children fall ill after eating mid-day meal in Saharsa
articles.summary: Over 150 children in Saharsa, Bihar, fell ill after consuming a mid-day meal at a government school. Villagers claimed a snake-like object was found in one student's plate, though school authorities did not confirm this. The incident raises concerns about food safety standards in India's mid-day meal scheme, a government welfare program providing free meals to schoolchildren. Health and education officials are likely to investigate the cause and prevent future occurrences.

articles.title: When Heritage Becomes a Market Category, Proof Must Follow the Launch
articles.summary: The Padma Doree initiative has launched a premium fabric line combining Eri silk and Chanderi, positioning itself as a sustainable and artisan-focused category. The initiative claims to prioritize sustainability and artisan value but lacks transparency on earnings distribution, decision-making, risk-sharing, and market demand durability. The article questions whether the premium benefits loom workers and calls for proof of sustainable impact. The initiative involves artisans and textile craftsma
articles.raw_summary: The just-launched Padma Doree initiative has been positioned as more than a fabric line, with Eri silk and Chanderi brought together inside a premium craft category that speaks of sustainability and artisan value, yet the claim will remain incomplete until the initiative shows who earns, who decides, who carries risk and whether the premium travels back to the loom through durable market demand.

These factors could impact consumer goods pricing and economic stability, potentially prompting government or regulatory responses.

Markets reacted with stock declines amid concerns over energy supply disruptions. India, a major oil importer, could face higher fuel costs and inflation if the situation escalates.

This trend raises concerns about broader economic stability and food security, which may prompt government or regulatory responses.

articles.title: Oil prices rise after US, Iran exchange fire at Strait of Hormuz
articles.summary: US and Iranian forces exchanged fire at the Strait of Hormuz, a key global oil shipping route. This marks the first clash since a ceasefire in April and has raised concerns about potential disruptions to oil supplies. The incident could impact global oil prices and energy markets, with implications for India's energy imports and trade policies.
articles.raw_summary: Oil prices rose after US and Iranian forces exchanged fire at the Strait of Hormuz, the first clash since the 7 April ceasefire, stoking fresh supply fears in a key global shipping lane.

articles.title: Oil prices steady as US, Iran consider fresh proposal for peace deal
articles.summary: Oil prices stabilized after an 8% drop as hopes of a US-Iran peace deal improved market sentiment. However, gains were limited due to conflicting signals from Donald Trump. The development could influence global oil supply and prices, which are critical for India's energy needs and economic stability.
articles.raw_summary: Oil prices steadied after an 8% drop as hopes of a US-Iran peace deal lifted sentiment, though conflicting signals from Donald Trump capped gains.

articles.title: India’s services sector recovers lost ground, domestic market fuels growth in April: HSBC India Services PMI
articles.summary: India's services sector showed recovery in April, with the HSBC PMI rising to 58.8. Increased output and new business orders were noted, alongside a shift to domestic suppliers. The composite PMI also rose to 58.2, indicating growth momentum across sectors.
articles.raw_summary: India's services sector showed recovery in April, with the HSBC PMI rising to 58.8. Increased output and new business orders were noted, alongside a shift to domestic suppliers. The composite PMI also rose to 58.2, indicating growth momentum across sectors.

articles.title: Amid Tamil Nadu govt formation drama, TVK says AMMK’s Kamaraj backed Vijay in letter, rebuts Dhinakaran’s claim
articles.summary: The article reports on a dispute within the AMMK party regarding support for Tamil Nadu Vikatan (TVK) leader Vijay during the state government formation. AMMK's MLA-elect Kamaraj S allegedly backed Vijay in a letter, which AMMK leader Dhinakaran called 'horse trading' and forgery. Dhinakaran reaffirmed his support for AIADMK's Edappadi K Palaniswami for the Chief Minister post, stating Kamaraj remains loyal to the NDA and AIADMK.
articles.raw_summary: Earlier, Dhinakaran claimed the letter supporting TVK is a case of ‘horse trading’ and forgery. He reaffirmed that AMMK's lone MLA-elect, Kamaraj S, remains loyal to the NDA and AIADMK. Dhinakaran officially backed AIADMK's Edappadi K Palaniswami (EPS) for the CM post.

articles.summary: The US Department of Defense's inspector general declined to investigate a US Army helicopter flight near musician Kid Rock's home and a protest against President Donald Trump. articles.title: Ukraine Looks Beyond Summer Offensive to a Crucial Winter in the War
articles.summary: Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelenskiy is urging Western allies to accelerate deliveries of air-defense systems and missiles to prepare for intensified Russian bombing during the upcoming winter. This follows a previous winter of heavy attacks on Ukrainian infrastructure. The request aims to bolster Kyiv's defenses against the ongoing Russian invasion.

articles.title: Mamata Banerjee loses Bengal: What next for TMC chief, a national role or state politics?
articles.summary: West Bengal Chief Minister Mamata Banerjee's Trinamool Congress (TMC) party lost the state elections, marking a stark contrast to 2021 when she returned to power via a bypoll. The article explores the implications of this defeat for Banerjee's political future, including whether she may shift focus to national politics or remain in state politics. The outcome could influence future governance decisions and party strategies in West Bengal.
articles.raw_summary: In 2021, Banerjee lost the Nandigram contest to Suvendu Adhikari. But her party had returned to power, allowing her to re-enter the Assembly through a bypoll from Bhabanipur. This time, however, the situation is starkly different from 2021. Her party has lost elections.

articles.title: Do you know what REITs are and how they work? A simple guide for investors

articles.summary: Suvankar Sen, MD & CEO of Senco Gold, said India’s gold demand is already showing signs of moderation due to elevated prices. Based on current trends, Sen estimates gold imports could decline to nearly 550 tonnes under a conservative scenario.

articles.summary: Over 250 children in Bihar’s Saharsa district fell ill after consuming a mid-day meal at a government school where a dead baby snake was reportedly found. The mid-day meal scheme is a government initiative aimed at improving child nutrition, and this incident raises concerns about its implementation and oversight.

articles.title: Most non-consensual intimate images being removed in two hours: officials
articles.summary: Social media platforms are now required to remove non-consensual intimate images and deepfakes within two hours of complaints, a significant improvement from the previous 24-hour deadline under updated IT rules. While compliance is high, challenges like false positives and cross-jurisdictional issues require human review, which can impact response times.

articles.title: India’s jobless rate rises to 5% in Q4
articles.summary: India's unemployment rate climbed to 5% in the January-March quarter. This rise was primarily due to an increase in joblessness among women. Overall urban unemployment saw a slight decrease, while rural unemployment edged up. Youth unemployment also experienced an upward trend during this period. Labour force participation and the worker population ratio saw minor dips.

articles.title: India’s urban unemployment rate eases to 6.6% in Jan-March quarter, rural joblessness rises to 4.3%
articles.summary: India's urban joblessness saw a slight dip to 6.6 percent in the first quarter of 2026. Rural unemployment, however, increased to 4.3 percent. Labour force participation rates also showed minor declines across urban and rural areas. Regular wage employment in rural regions grew, while self-employment decreased. Agriculture's share in rural jobs reduced as tertiary and secondary sectors expanded.

Global prices are soaring.

articles.title: For Agra’s footwear artisans, the west Asia war is more than just sand in the shoe
articles.summary: The ongoing war in West Asia is disrupting Agra’s footwear industry, where artisans and exporters face rising costs and delayed supplies. Orders are harder to fulfill on time, squeezing profit margins and threatening recovery even if global tensions ease. The industry warns it may take at least a season to bounce back. Workers and businesses are bearing the brunt of these supply chain disruptions.

With margins thinning and orders getting harder to execute on time, the industry says it may take at least a season to recover even if global tensions ease.

articles.title: Oil jumps as Strait of Hormuz stays shut after Trump rebuffs Iran’s offer
articles.summary: Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu stated in an interview that the war with Iran is 'not over,' amid ongoing tensions in the Strait of Hormuz, a critical oil shipping route. The closure of the strait could disrupt global oil supplies, impacting energy prices and India's fuel imports. The situation involves Iran, Israel, and the U.S., with broader implications for regional stability and India's energy security.

articles.title: The acid attack survivor who forces us to look
articles.summary: Shaheen Malik, an acid attack survivor, lost her personal legal battle for justice but this week secured a landmark Supreme Court ruling that expands legal protections for acid attack survivors nationwide. The ruling strengthens survivor rights and could lead to broader policy changes in handling such crimes. The case highlights systemic gaps in justice for vulnerable groups and may prompt further legislative or administrative reforms.

articles.title: CM Vijay’s oath ceremony sparks CPI, DMK outrage over Thamizhthai Vaazhthu: What is the controversy about?
articles.summary: Tamil Nadu's new Chief Minister Vijay was sworn in, promising a secular government. However, the CPI and DMK criticized the order of the state song 'Tamil Thaai Vaazhthu' during the ceremony, demanding it be prioritized in all future government events. The controversy centers on the song's placement and its symbolic importance in state functions. The opposition parties argue this reflects a deviation from secular governance.
